sections.heading: The Activity: The Entry Point
sections.content: In Android, an Activity represents a single screen with a user interface. Every Activity must inherit from AppCompatActivity.

sections.heading: Basic Activity Structure
Important Methods
items: onCreate(): Called when the Activity is first created.
setContentView(): Connects the Activity to its XML layout file.

sections.heading: Accessing Views (XML to Kotlin)
sections.content: To interact with UI elements defined in XML, they must be referenced inside Kotlin code.

sections.heading: findViewById (Traditional Method)
sections.content: This method is built into the Android SDK and manually connects views using their ID.

sections.heading: findViewById Example
View Binding (Recommended)
sections.content: View Binding generates a binding class for each XML layout. It is safer and reduces the risk of NullPointerExceptions.

sections.heading: View Binding Example
sections.code:
private lateinit var binding: ActivityMainBinding

override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
    super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
    binding = ActivityMainBinding.inflate(layoutInflater)
    setContentView(binding.root)

    binding.btnSubmit.text = "Click Me"
}

sections.heading: The lateinit Keyword
sections.content: lateinit var is used for variables that are initialized later, typically inside onCreate instead of the constructor.

sections.heading: Handling Click Listeners with Lambdas
sections.content: Kotlin allows concise click handling using lambda expressions and trailing lambda syntax.

sections.heading: Logcat for Debugging
sections.content: The Log class is used to print debug information and track application behavior.

sections.heading: Log Example
sections.code:
Log.d("MainActivity", "User clicked the button")
Log.e("MainActivity", "Error loading data")

sections.heading: String Resources & Context
sections.content: Strings should not be hardcoded. Use getString() to retrieve values from strings.xml.

sections.heading: Summary
items: Activities represent screens in an Android app.
Use View Binding instead of findViewById for safer UI access.
lateinit allows delayed initialization of variables.
Use Log for debugging and getString() for resource management.
